title: Filling in Missing Parts of an Image
concept: Inpainting fills in a masked or missing region of an image with newly generated content that blends naturally with the surrounding pixels.

exercises.prompt: You have a photo with a torn corner missing. What would 'fixing' it mean?

options.text: Filling in the torn part so it looks whole again
Making the photo black and white

exercises.explain: That's exactly what inpainting does — it fills in missing or damaged parts of an image so it looks complete!

cards.title: Oops, Part of the Picture is Gone!
cards.body: Imagine a photo of your dog with a big scribble covering its face. Could an AI guess what should be there and paint it back in — perfectly? That's the magic of inpainting!

cards.title: What is Inpainting?
cards.body: Inpainting is when an AI fills in a missing or masked (covered up) part of an image with new content it creates. The new part is made to blend smoothly with the colors, shapes, and style of the rest of the picture.

cards.title: The Missing Puzzle Piece
cards.body: Think of a jigsaw puzzle with one piece missing. Instead of leaving a hole, an artist studies the picture around the gap and paints a brand new piece that fits perfectly — matching colors and lines so you'd never know it wasn't part of the original puzzle.

cards.title: Removing a Photobomber
cards.body: Say you took a beach photo but a stranger walked into the shot. You erase (mask) that person, and an inpainting AI fills the empty space with more sand and waves — matching the lighting and texture so it looks like the person was never there.

cards.title: Myth: It Just Copies Nearby Pixels
cards.body: Some think inpainting simply stretches or copies nearby pixels to cover the gap. Actually, modern AI inpainting generates brand-new content by understanding the whole scene, so it can even add believable new details, not just repeat patterns.

cards.title: Masked vs. Inpainted
cards.body: Here's the difference between an image with a missing region and the same image after AI inpainting fills it in.

left.text: A gray or black blank patch covers part of the image, hiding details.

right.text: The patch is replaced with new, matching content that blends seamlessly with the rest of the image.

exercises.prompt: What does inpainting do to an image?

options.text: Fills in a missing or masked region with new, matching content
Turns the image into black and white
Makes the image bigger without changing content

exercises.explain: Inpainting specifically targets a missing or covered area and fills it with newly generated content that blends with the rest of the image.

exercises.prompt: Inpainting can only copy pixels from right next to the missing area, not create anything new.
exercises.explain: False! Modern inpainting AI understands the whole image and generates brand-new, believable content, not just a copy of nearby pixels.

exercises.sentence: Inpainting fills in a ___ region of an image with new content that blends naturally.
exercises.explain: The masked (or missing) region is exactly the part inpainting focuses on filling in smoothly.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the BEST example of inpainting in action?

options.text: An AI removes a fence from a photo and fills the gap with matching grass and sky
An AI converts a color photo into a sketch drawing
An AI translates text written on a sign into another language

exercises.explain: Removing the fence and filling the empty space with realistic matching grass and sky is a classic inpainting task — filling a gap seamlessly.

exercises.prompt: A photo editing app lets you erase a scratch on an old picture, and the app automatically paints in what was likely behind the scratch, matching the surrounding colors and patterns. What AI technique is this app using?
exercises.explain: This is inpainting — the AI fills in the missing/damaged region (the scratch) with newly generated content that naturally matches the surrounding image.
